title: Cell Tower / BTS Connectivity Loss
symptoms: One or more cell towers stops sending keepalives to OSS/BSS
Subscriber complaints about no signal in the affected coverage area
Backhaul microwave or fiber link to the site is down
steps: Ping the tower management IP to check basic reachability
Check the backhaul circuit status in the NMS (microwave hop or fiber segment)
SSH to the aggregation router serving the tower: 'show interfaces' for the tower-facing port
Check if the issue is isolated to one tower or an entire cluster (sector fault vs backhaul fault)
Verify the OSS/BSS alarm count — single tower vs regional outage indicates different root causes
Check power status at the site if remote monitoring is available (generator, mains, rectifier)
Attempt remote reboot of the eNodeB through the EMS if backhaul is up but BTS is silent
Dispatch field team if remote recovery fails or if power/physical damage is suspected
resolution: If backhaul is down: fix the transport (see RB-NET-003 for link flapping). If BTS is unresponsive with backhaul up: remote reboot via EMS. If power failure: coordinate with field team for generator refuel or rectifier replacement. Document coverage impact and notify Customer Care for proactive communication.
escalation: If more than 3 towers are down in the same area (possible transport node failure), escalate to Transport Network team immediately. P1 if coverage gap affects >10,000 subscribers.
estimated_resolution_time: 30 minutes to 4 hours (depending on field dispatch)
